titleStmt.title: SII 26.315: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 315. (A.R. No. 297 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription describes the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same temple of Tirukkachchūr occurring in the above-discussed inscriptions, by Kulālaṉ Kākkunāyakkaṉ, a karaṇattāṉ and owner of property in the village. It further records that a sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu was endowed by him for this purpose, and this money was accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Kāñchikuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Uḍayapiḷḷai alias Nityakalyāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra], who was the Śivāchārya of the temple and who had (1/4) share in the right of worship in the same temple. It is further stated that this bhaṭṭa had agreed to burn the sandhi lamp perpetually.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiruvāykeḻvi muṉṉāka tribhuvanaccakravattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyuṅkoṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottu
ṅkacoḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu 10 5 Āvatu tirukkaccūr Uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyārkku Ivvūrkkāṇi Uṭaiya karaṇattāṉ kulālaṉ
kākkunāyakaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku Oṉṟukku Ikkoyiṟ śaivācāryyamum Ikkoyiṟ kāṇinālattoṉṟum Uṭaiya śivabrāhmaṇaṉ
bhāradvāji kādvikkuṟi śrīkaṇṭhabhaṭṭaṉ Uṭaipiḷḷaiyāṉa nittakalyāṇabhaṭṭaṉ Ivar pakkaṟ kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu 2 Ippaḻaṅkācu Iraṇṭu
m kaikkoṇṭu Iccantiviḷakku candrādityavarai Erikkak kaṭaveṉāka sammatittuccilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ Uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyāṉa ni
tyakalyāṇabhaṭṭaṉeṉ Itu paṉmāheśvararakṣai §
Digital edition of SII 26.315 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
